Under the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS), entities that exceed their specified emission reduction targets can generate carbon credits. Who is the designated Regulator for the CCTS scheme, responsible for regulatory oversight and enforcement?
options: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC).

Key Points

  • The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC) is the designated regulator for the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS).
  • CERC is responsible for ensuring regulatory oversight, compliance, and enforcement related to carbon credit trading activities in India.
  • The CCTS enables entities to trade carbon credits, which are generated when they exceed their emission reduction targets.
  • This scheme aligns with global efforts to curb greenhouse gas emissions and promotes a market-based mechanism to address climate change.
  • CERC also ensures transparency, fairness, and accountability in the carbon trading market while fostering sustainable development.

Additional Information

  • Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE):
    • BEE is a statutory body under the Ministry of Power that promotes energy efficiency and conservation in India.
    • It develops energy-saving policies, implements energy efficiency programs, and certifies energy-efficient products through initiatives like the Perform, Achieve, and Trade (PAT) scheme.
    • While BEE is involved in emission reduction efforts, it is not the designated regulator for the CCTS.
  • Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI):
    • SEBI is the regulatory authority for securities markets in India.
    • It oversees stock exchanges, mutual funds, and other financial instruments but does not regulate carbon credit trading under CCTS.
    • SEBI’s primary focus is on protecting investors and ensuring transparency in financial markets.
  • Reserve Bank of India (RBI):
    • The RBI is India’s central banking institution that regulates monetary policy, banking operations, and financial stability in the country.
    • While it plays a crucial role in the economy, the RBI is not responsible for regulating carbon credit trading under the CCTS.
